cards.rules_text:
[i]Exploring[/i] — Mezzan enters with an additional loyalty counter on it for each differently named land you control.
[+1]: You gain 2 life.
[-3]: Create a tapped token that's a copy of target land you control.
[-5]: Put two +1/+1 counters on each land you control. They become 0/0 creatures until your next turn.
cards.notes: Multicolor silver planeswalkers

cards.rules_text:
When Cave of Oracles enters, you get {VU}. [i](Spend {V} to cast copies of Vertex spells.)[/i]
Whenever your complexity is three or greater, up to one other target permanent you control becomes a 0/0 creature until end of turn. Put two +1/+1 counters on it. This ability triggers only once each turn. [i](Your complexity is the number of spells you've cast and nonland abilities you've activated this turn.)[/i]
cards.notes:
A silver value engine that isn't based on tokens.
Remeber to keep pie weaknesses in mind, the color pie doc is at https://docs.google.com/document/d/1d8gmoeJz7j3ZO6l0zu6vjXwidv88Y7WifQ5sCcxfKe4/edit?tab=t.wrmowdyu4sws
https://www.artstation.com/artwork/gPRG

cards.rules_text:
When Mind Trinity enters or attacks, if it's not a token, create a token that's a copy of target creature, then exile that token.
Warp {1}{I} [i](You may cast this card from your hand for its warp cost. Exile this creature at the beginning of the next end step, then you may cast it from exile on a later turn.)[/i]
cards.notes:
A silver topend threat at mana value 5 or greater. This shouldn't be able to take over the game on its own for pie reasons.
Remeber to keep pie weaknesses in mind, the color pie doc is at https://docs.google.com/document/d/1d8gmoeJz7j3ZO6l0zu6vjXwidv88Y7WifQ5sCcxfKe4/edit?tab=t.wrmowdyu4sws

cards.rules_text:

[+1]: The next time target creature you control deals combat damage to a player, draw a card.
[-1]: Create a 2/1 blue Spirit creature token with flying.
[-2]: Tap target creature and put a stun counter on it. For as long as it remains tapped, it loses all abilities.

cards.notes:

Silver vertex production

Custom symbol is linked in set info
V for 1 vertex
V/W is for 2
V/U is for 3
V/B is for 4
V/R is for 5
V/G is the plus used in vertex "adventure" costs

cards.rules_text:
If one or more +1/+1 counters would be put on a creature you control, that many plus one +1/+1 counters are put on it instead.

[-3]: Draw a card for each creature you control with power greater than its base power.
[-5]: Put two +1/+1 counters on each creature you control.
